case_for: Woods has shown occasional volume spikes (6.4 pts in wk9) suggesting some week-to-week upside if targets increase or he finds the end zone, but there's little evidence supporting a major role change.
case_against: Woods has consistently produced low-volume, low-impact performances (all six recent games under 6.5 pts, averaging roughly 3 pts) with a pregame projection of just 1.8 points, indicating a marginal role with minimal scoring ceiling.
